title: Mastercard expands card settlement hours via stablecoins
snippet: Mastercard has announced the use of stablecoins to enable intraday and weekend settlement between card issuers and acquirers. title: Tether launching tokenized gold Visa card with XAUT rewards
snippet: The card will operate on the Visa network, enabling users to spend fiat at merchant stores worldwide where Visa cards are accepted.

title: SEC makes digital assets strategic priority through 2030
snippet: The regulator’s five-year roadmap calls for clearer crypto rules, support for tokenization and a framework for staking and onchain markets.

title: UK Regulator Warns Soccer Clubs Over Unauthorized Crypto Sponsorship Deals
snippet: The FCA has warned Premier League clubs that partnerships with unlicensed crypto firms could expose fans to unregulated trading platforms.

title: Crypto firms face July 1 EU cutoff as MiCA grace period ends
snippet: The ESMA says crypto companies without MiCA authorization must stop serving EU clients from July 1, even if their licence applications remain under review.

title: Mastercard and Visa Back Stealth Stablecoin Platform
snippet: Mastercard, Stripe and Visa are among the backers of a stablecoin platform that will soon be introduced, CoinDesk reported Wednesday (June 3), citing unnamed sources. Coinbase is considering participating in the platform, the report said, citing one of the unnamed sources. The report included no fur

title: Russian ruble stablecoin A7A5 grows despite Western sanctions: CertiK
snippet: Russian ruble-backed stablecoin A7A5 processed over $110 billion in transactions despite Western sanctions, according to CertiK.

title: RedotPay enters B2B market
snippet: RedotPay, a global stablecoin-based payment fintech company serving over 7 million users across 100+ countries, today announced the launch of RedotPay Connect at Money20/20 Europe, marking the company’s first dedicated B2B product since inception and a major step into enterprise payments.

snippet: The exchange has invested an undisclosed amount in ProShares’ Treasury-focused ETF built for the post-GENIUS era as lawmakers debate whether stablecoin issuers can offer yield-bearing products.

title: Revolut Wants to Give US Banking Customers Stablecoin Access
snippet: Revolut reportedly plans to include stablecoins as part of its U.S. banking offering. That’s according to Revolut U.S. CEO Cetin Duransoy, interviewed Wednesday (June 3) by Reuters.
